behavior: Clings upright to trunks and large branches, bracing with stiff tail feathers; probes, scales bark, and excavates dead wood for insects.
field_marks: Medium-sized, boldly black-and-white woodpecker with an upright, straight-backed posture.
Long, straight, chisel-like bill, nearly as long as the head.
Two white facial stripes frame a black head; males show a small red patch on the rear crown.
Broad white patch runs down the center of the black back.
Black wings are checkered with white; outer tail feathers are plain white and stiff.
Dark eye and feet; long, pointed tail used as a brace on trunks.
habitat: Mature deciduous, coniferous, and mixed woodlands with medium to large trees; also forest edges, groves, burned stands, and other treed habitats with dead wood.
